data.description:
# Make the API-stability version reference version-agnostic (remove the drift-prone marker)

Adopt the mainstream OSS pattern (FastAPI/attrs/httpx): state the stability **policy**
without naming a current version in prose, and point readers to the CHANGELOG / GitHub
Releases for the live number. This removes the duplicated `currently X.Y.Z` marker in
`docs/api-stability.md` — and with it the drift it caused and the guard that policed it
(`tests/unit/test_version_sync.py`) — so no version must be hand-bumped in the doc each
release. (Design Option A from the api-stability version-marker analysis; Option E —
VCS-derived versioning — is parked separately as an idea.)

## Why

`docs/api-stability.md` hard-codes `(currently 0.8.0)`, duplicating `pyproject.toml`'s
version. That duplication silently drifted before (the doc once read 0.6.0 while the
package was 0.7.1), which is why `tests/unit/test_version_sync.py` exists to fail CI on a
mismatch. But the specific patch number adds nothing over the "0.x ⇒ any minor may break"
statement that carries the real meaning; the live version belongs in the CHANGELOG /
Releases, which the release flow already maintains. Removing the marker eliminates the
duplication at its source, so the consistency test is correctly removed WITH it (removing
the test while keeping the marker would reopen the drift bug). Popular actively-maintained
projects write their pre-1.0 stability policy this way: FastAPI's "About FastAPI versions"
and attrs' "Backward Compatibility" name no current version and defer to the changelog.

## What / Scope

- `docs/api-stability.md` — reword the opening so it states the 0.x SemVer policy WITHOUT a
  `currently X.Y.Z` version literal, and links to the CHANGELOG and GitHub Releases for the
  current version. Keep the rest of the page (the per-surface stability matrix) unchanged.
- `tests/unit/test_version_sync.py` — DELETE. Its sole purpose was to keep the now-removed
  marker in sync; with no marker there is nothing to check.
- `docs/releasing.md` — remove the two references added when cutting v0.8.0 that now dangle:
  drop `docs/api-stability.md` from the step-1 `git add` bump list and delete the
  `test_version_sync.py`/`(currently X.Y.Z)` mentions, so the runbook no longer tells a
  future release-cutter to bump a marker that no longer exists.
- Out of scope: any change to `pyproject.toml`/`server.json` version handling (that is
  Option E, parked as an idea).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/api-stability.md` contains NO `currently <X.Y.Z>` version literal (verify:
  `! grep -Eq 'currently [0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' docs/api-stability.md`) and DOES link to the
  changelog/releases for the version (verify: `grep -Eq 'CHANGELOG|releases' docs/api-stability.md`).
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_version_sync.py` no longer exists (verify:
  `test ! -e tests/unit/test_version_sync.py`).
- [ ] `docs/releasing.md` no longer references `test_version_sync` or a `currently X.Y.Z`
  marker, and its step-1 `git add` list no longer includes `docs/api-stability.md` (verify:
  `! grep -q 'test_version_sync' docs/releasing.md && ! grep -q 'api-stability' docs/releasing.md`).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass (the deleted test is gone; nothing else
  references it), and the change lands on `main` through the Gerrit two-vote gate.
data.title: Make API-stability version reference version-agnostic (remove drift-prone marker + test)
